perturbation.original_text: 1.5. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall mean, subject to the provisions of Section 7.2 [EXCLUSIONS], all proprietary and confidential information of a party, including, without limitation, trade secrets, technical information, business information, sales information, customer and potential customer lists and identities, product sales plans, sublicense agreements, inventions, developments, discoveries, software, know-how, methods, techniques, formulae, data, processes and other trade secrets and proprietary ideas, whether or not protectable under patent, trademark, copyright or other areas of law, that the other party has access to or receives. For purposes of this Agreement, 1 * Represents confidential information for which Impresse Corporation is seeking confidential treatment with the Securities and Exchange Commission. the Co-Branded Content shall not be considered Confidential Information of Impresse. For purposes of this Agreement, this Agreement shall be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall mean, subject to the provisions of Section 7.2 [EXCLUSIONS], all proprietary and confidential information of a party, including, without limitation, trade secrets, technical information, business information, sales information, customer and potential customer lists and identities, product sales plans, sublicense agreements, inventions, developments, discoveries, software, know-how, methods, techniques, formulae, data, processes and other trade secrets and proprietary ideas, whether or not protectable under patent, trademark, copyright or other areas of law, that the other party has access to or receives. For purposes of this Agreement, 1 * Represents confidential information for which Impresse Corporation is seeking confidential treatment with the Securities and Exchange Commission. the Co-Branded Content shall be considered Confidential Information of Impresse. For purposes of this Agreement, this Agreement shall be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Co-Branded Content' is initially defined as not being considered Confidential Information of Impresse. By modifying this to state that 'Co-Branded Content shall be considered Confidential Information of Impresse', this directly contradicts the original definition. This will now cause uncertainty in Section 7 (Confidentiality) specifically around the obligations of maintaining and disclosing such Co-Branded content, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1. CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS. Except as permitted elsewhere under this Agreement, each party agrees to take Reasonable Steps (as defined below) (a) to receive and maintain the Confidential Information of the other party in confidence and (b) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties, provided, the receiving party may disclose such Confidential Information to its employees, representatives and agents who have a need to know such information for purposes of carrying out the terms of this Agreement. Neither party hereto shall use all or any part of the Confidential Information of the other party for any purpose other than to perform its obligations under this Agreement. The parties will take Reasonable Steps (as defined below) to ensure that their employees, representatives and agents comply with this provision. As used herein, "Reasonable Steps" means at least the same degree of care that the receiving party uses to protect its own Confidential Information, and, in no event, not less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Co-Branded Content shall not be considered Confidential Information of Impresse.' The changed text modifies this to 'Co-Branded Content shall be considered Confidential Information of Impresse.' This directly contradicts the original statement. Section 7.1 outlines confidentiality obligations for 'Confidential Information.' By changing the definition of what constitutes Confidential Information, the change directly impacts and contradicts the obligations outlined in Section 7.1, creating uncertainty and a direct conflict regarding how Co-Branded Content is to be treated under the confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1. The Term of this Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall end fifteen months therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1. The Term of this Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall end fifteen months therefrom, unless otherwise agreed upon by VerticalNet.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless otherwise agreed upon by VerticalNet' to the end of the term definition, this creates an in-text contradiction with the original fixed term. Now, Impresse may expect the contract to end after 15 months, but VerticalNet has the power to unilaterally extend it, conflicting with mutual agreements under contract law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.7. AMENDMENT OR MODIFICATION. No subsequent amendment, modification or waiver of any of the provisions of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a fixed 15-month term. The changed text introduces an exception allowing VerticalNet to unilaterally extend the term, which contradicts the clause requiring amendments or modifications to be in writing and signed by both parties (Section 10.7). This unilateral change creates a strong in-text contradiction, making the contract's term ambiguous and potentially unenforceable as written.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1.2. Except for the express rights granted to VerticalNet under this Agreement, VerticalNet acknowledges and agrees that the Intellectual Property of Impresse is and shall remain the sole property of Impresse and nothing in this Agreement shall confer in VerticalNet any right of ownership or license rights in Impresse's Intellectual Property. In addition, VerticalNet shall not now or in the future contest the validity of Impresse's ownership of its Intellectual Property; provided, however, that VerticalNet may contest the validity of Impresse's Intellectual Property in any proceeding brought against VerticalNet alleging infringement or misappropriation of Impresse's Intellectual Property.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1.2. Except for the express rights granted to VerticalNet under this Agreement, VerticalNet acknowledges and agrees that the Intellectual Property of Impresse is and shall remain the sole property of Impresse and nothing in this Agreement shall confer in VerticalNet any right of ownership or license rights in Impresse's Intellectual Property. VerticalNet may, in the future, contest the validity of Impresse's ownership of its Intellectual Property; provided, however, that VerticalNet may contest the validity of Impresse's Intellectual Property in any proceeding brought against VerticalNet alleging infringement or misappropriation of Impresse's Intellectual Property.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'In addition, VerticalNet shall not now or in the future contest the validity of Impresse's ownership of its Intellectual Property; provided, however, that', it now directly contradicts the clause that VerticalNet acknowledge Impresse's IP. This is because it now allows VerticalNet to contest Impresse's Intellectual Property at any point, creating uncertainty and an in-text contradiction within the same section. This can cause issues related to section 8 and 9 regarding Representation, warranties, disclaimers and limitation of liabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1.2. Except for the express rights granted to VerticalNet under this Agreement, VerticalNet acknowledges and agrees that the Intellectual Property of Impresse is and shall remain the sole property of Impresse and nothing in this Agreement shall confer in VerticalNet any right of ownership or license rights in Impresse's Intellectual Property. VerticalNet may, in the future, contest the validity of Impresse's ownership of its Intellectual Property; provided, however, that VerticalNet may contest the validity of Impresse's Intellectual Property in any proceeding brought against VerticalNet alleging infringement or misappropriation of Impresse's Intellectual Property.
perturbation.justification: The change removes the explicit prohibition on VerticalNet contesting Impresse's IP validity, which creates a direct conflict with the initial acknowledgement and agreement in section 10.1.2 that Impresse's IP is and shall remain its sole property. The changed text directly contradicts the purpose of the contradicted text, which is also section 10.1.2, leading to uncertainty about the enforceability of Impresse's IP rights.
